text: Training semantic parsers for large-scale knowledge bases such as Freebase has generally required expensive logical form annotations. We present a method for semantic parser training from only question-answer pairs, making the approach more applicable to open-domain question answering scenarios. We address the predicate space explosion issue with two novel ideas: 1) we train a coarse parser that maps linguistic phrases to predicates in Freebase using a large amount of text and Freebase itself as training data; 2) we train a “bridging” parser that finds implicit relationships between phrases by predicting predicates that can link any two logical forms. Our parser achieves 62% accuracy on our semantic parser evaluation dataset, while using less supervision than prior work. We also introduce a new dataset named WEBQUESTIONS that consists of a large set of crowdsourced question answers to search engine queries. The results show that our parser can indeed handle the great variability of natural language expressions present in real-world human queries.
explanation: The passage reads like a coherent academic abstract with domain-specific terminology and plausible dataset references (Freebase, WebQuestions), consistent with human authorship; no obvious signs of typical AI-generated text.
